Sludge. Itâs not exactly super sexy, but what you do with it is important.

Brewing creates three main organic waste streams: malt, hops and yeast.
Our spent malt already gets repurposed as animal feed (and occasional crackers and dog biscuits), but sludge (the thick slurry of leftover hop material and yeast) has historically been a trickier problem to solve.
For a long time, we collected it in tanks, but it still ended up being trucked to landfill as liquid waste. Not great.

Enter: The Sludge Tank.

In 2024, funding from the Wellington City Council Waste Minimisation Seed Fund - Organics Diversion Fund allowed us to build a proper system, designed by our own Steve Almond (Sustainability & Projects Manager) in collaboration with EnviroNZ. Sludge is now pumped from our fermenters into a holding tank, collected by EnviroNZ, and composted by Capital Compost, turning it into potting mix and soil for public use.

The impact?

 ð± 60+ tonnes of organic waste diverted from landfill per year
 ð± 5+ tonnes of CO2 emissions prevented

Very cool, very satisfying. End of story, right?....Wrong!

 After 8 months of doing this, we noticed somethingâ¦ the sludge smells incredible! Concentrated hop aroma, bright, fruity, punchy. Too good to compost, and you know that here at Garage Project, we're always wondering if things can be made into beer, and this sludge was no different. We gave hope a chance, and we are so thrilled to announce that our recycling experiment was a delicious success!

Introducing:

Sludgefest, Radical Recycled Hop IPA - 9.5%

This tasty beer is brewed by giving those leftover dry hops a second life before they make their noble exit to compost. Multiple doses of leftover dry hop âsludgeâ, still loaded with hop aroma and flavour, pumped straight from other beers into this tank of Sludgefest making it technically our most heavily hopped beer ever. Wasteâs only waste if you waste it, and, trust us, You wonât want to waste a drop.

ð° GAYâS CREAMERY, DAWLISH ð°

Celebrating their one hundredth birthday in business this year is the wildly popular Gayâs Creamery in Dawlish. 

Serving a wide variety of cakes, pasties, Cheesy Steves, cream teas and their ever popular ice cream with a big dollop of clotted cream on top! 

To celebrate their centenary, this beautiful vintage Bedford van is sitting outside the shop and it looks magnificent. Have you been to see it yet? 

Dawlish is full of wonderful independent family-run businesses, itâs what makes this town so very special. So make sure you come to Dawlish soon and enjoy the unique shopping experience and a plethora of tasty food! 

Fancy holidaying in Dawlish next year? Take a look at Pilchard Cottage here ð http://www.sykescottages.co.uk/cottage/Devon-Dawlish/Pilchard-Cottage-1143949.html

messages.content: Was always fine, the worst night was the entire bag of chocolate coated Coffee Beans

Yerp, self medication is gonna happen if you donât know whatâs up and how to dealðAnd I was gonna say wow but on a night shift I could do that many coffeesð havenât done it in a while though.
Legit 30 coffees a day didn't help.

Because my body still 'needed' a stimulant to relax and be 'like everyone else' but I didn't know that at the time, and sugar was the fix. I self medicated for about 10 years until I realised what i was doing.

Then another 10 years later, and they decided 'Whooar whoooar, we've given GP's too much control over these diagnoses' people with Autism, Bipolar, all kinds of other similar closely related spectrums were being stuffed with ADHD drugs. Which is insanely bad... I ended up with a sugar addiction when I was about 20... trying to ween myself off the ADHD meds after I found out I was more likely Aspergers.
20 years later they hand a book to GPâs and say âAhh just take a guess.â
When I was diagnosed it was a legitimate 3 month adventure, they would wire you up, to EEG and study your brain function with stimuli and medicate the effects, with samples.
Dr Serfontein. He was great! Until he died in South Africa on a hunting trip of Pneumonia.
Back when I got diagnosed my doctor was the actual legitimate person who coined the term ADD.
She would not even ever suggest that she is actually borderline, she found a doctor in her Bipolar era, so that she could be only bipolar. ðð¤¦ð¼

Iâm often saying Iâm not a doctor but can quite confidently feel Iâm right about a diagnosis with some people. Iâm just realising how fucking expensive itâs going to be to get a diagnosis for adhd ð°âï¸ð¥ð¤¦ð»ââï¸
Not at all. You donât have to be a paid specialist to know or notice things about people. I have known a couple of people with borderline and know a bit from them and a bit from the literature.
I probably sound like an asshole. ð¬
Iâm not a psych so I canât diagnose or legitimise anything that Iâve just said in any way, but itâs just what has occurred.

Having known her for as long as I have itâs hard to explain to people that Iâve know her over all of her personalities, because by the time she has a new one sheâll cut everyone off from the old one and if you mention any of it, she just goes weird like âWhaaaaaaaaatâ itâs uncanny. But sheâs good and awesome, in most cases. Occasionally not so much.
Sometimes itâs hard to be her friend. But if I didnât no one else would. She cuts people off for breakfast.

Iâve known her since she was about 14
I know that sheâs borderline personality, and she has a friend who changed her nameâ¦ and she had to fit in.

share.share_text:
What a week, maybe we have something for you... Although it's dark and freezing cold in Finland for almost half the year, the people there are among the happiest in the world. First place in the World Happiness Report seven times in a row - what is their secret? 

Perhaps it's the high quality of life, the endless forests or the many saunas. But certainly also because of KalsarikÃ¤nnit. Sounds complicated, but means something like: âGetting drunk at home in your knickersâ. 

A word that perfectly summarises how you can experience maximum enjoyment with minimum effort - alone, relaxed, no frills. No wonder this philosophy has so much to do with Finland's relaxed attitude to life. Instead of the eternal pursuit of perfection, Finns indulge in pleasure, irrationality and a little intoxication. 

Perhaps this is precisely the antithesis of our optimisation mania: simply letting go and celebrating life. Is KalsarikÃ¤nnit perhaps something for you?

In 1982, the song âMoscow Nightsâ gained international recognition when American pianist and entertainer Van Cliburn performed it during his tour in the Soviet Union. 

The song, originally titled âÐÐ¾Ð´Ð¼Ð¾ÑÐºÐ¾Ð²Ð½ÑÐµ Ð²ÐµÑÐµÑÐ°â (âPodmoskovnye Vecheraâ), was composed in 1955 by Vasily Solovyov-Sedoi with lyrics by Mikhail Matusovsky. It became one of the most famous Russian songs worldwide.

âMoscow Nightsâ had already been a popular tune in the Soviet Union, celebrated for its nostalgic and poetic lyrics about the peaceful beauty of Moscowâs surroundings. 

The song became a symbol of Soviet culture and was often used at international events. Van Cliburnâs 1982 performance of the song, as part of his cultural diplomacy during the Cold War, helped further cement its status as a bridge between the U.S. and the Soviet Union through music. The song remains one of Russiaâs most recognizable and beloved melodies to this day.

share.share_text:
Follow @entreprneurinsights for more insights like this

Potato Parcel is a quirky and unique service that allows people to send custom messages or images printed on potatoes, making for a humorous and memorable gift.

Founded in 2015, it gained widespread attention after appearing on Shark Tank, where entrepreneur Mark Cuban invested in the business. 

The concept is simple yet entertaining: users choose a potato, add their personalized message or photo, and the company delivers it to the recipient.

Potato Parcel has become a viral sensation, proving that even the most unconventional ideas can find success in niche markets.

Credits: @potatoparcel @usatoday 

#entrepreneur #entrepreneurmindset #entrepreneurlife #entrepreneurship #entrepreneurs
